goal: Add a deterministic intermediary receipt coverage contract that classifies realtime, browser, compaction, mailbox, streamed status, and other intermediary state as orientation unless a current receipt, source freshness, head binding, and claim-support policy make it eligible.

forbiddenFiles: GitHub, CodeRabbit, Linear, CircleCI, release, merge, or destructive cleanup mutations
public command authority changes beyond a narrow standalone validator
delivery-truth pass-claim promotion from realtime or intermediary state in this slice
raw browser DOM dumps, screenshots, transcripts, streamed tool payloads, mailbox bodies, full compaction summaries, secrets, tokens, credentials, or unredacted environment values in packet payloads

designConstraints: Intermediary receipt coverage must live in a deep module under src/lib/intermediary-receipts, with a narrow validator script wrapper.

The packet must cover intermediary surfaces that frequently mislead agents: browser state, streamed status, mailbox/subagent status text, compaction summaries, current UI screenshots, realtime event snippets, external check snapshots, and operator steering echoes.

Intermediary sources are orientation by default. Claim support requires a referenced evidence-receipt/v1 with evidenceUse claim_support, status pass, freshness current, verifiedAt, current head SHA when head-bound, source freshness current, and explicit policy eligibility for the target claim.

Unbound intermediary orientation is allowed only as low-confidence navigation evidence when the source is pointer-only, redacted, non-raw, bounded, and marked ineligible for claim support. Receipt-bound orientation is preferred, but lack of a receipt must not force fake receipt generation for navigation-only state.
Claim policy must be deny-by-default. Every sourceKind and claimFamily combination that is not explicitly allowlisted must be ineligible with a machine-readable policy_matrix_denied blocker.

Intermediary sources must not satisfy canonical external-state, review-state, merge-readiness, Linear-alignment, or Judge/PM claim families unless the source is a canonical packet ref from that family and the receipt proves that canonical packet; an intermediary snapshot cannot impersonate external-state or review-state authority.

Mixed-source aggregation must be most-restrictive-wins for each claim family: fail or blocked sources outrank warnings, warnings outrank pass, head mismatches outrank stale state, stale state outranks missing optional source details, and blockers must be preserved rather than cherry-picked away.

The required intermediary taxonomy is browser_state, streamed_status, mailbox_status, compaction_summary, screenshot_or_visual_state, realtime_event_snippet, external_check_snapshot, operator_steering_echo, and subagent_status_text. Fixtures must exercise every source kind so a partial implementation cannot silently pass SPG-012.

Each blockerClass must map to a deterministic nextActionClass such as refresh_receipt, capture_hash, rebind_head, route_to_canonical_packet, request_policy_exception, redact_source, or use_orientation_only; unknown nextActionClass values must fail validation.

Mailbox text, browser page presence, screenshots, remembered summaries, and compaction history must be rejected as claim support unless they point to a current receipt-bound artifact with path, hash, producer, freshness, and allowed claim family.

The validator must separate source status from claim eligibility so realtime orientation can still guide next action while closeout, merge readiness, review addressed, Linear alignment, Judge/PM readiness, and delivery-truth pass claims fail closed.
The packet must reject raw or bulky payloads and store only bounded source refs, receipt refs, hashes, freshness, status, claim families, blockers, and next actions.
Head-bound intermediary evidence must fail claim eligibility when observedHeadSha and currentHeadSha differ.
Expired, stale, missing, unknown, unverifiable, external-only, or orientation-only intermediary sources must produce machine-readable blockers rather than ambiguous warnings.
Schema, TypeScript constants, example, semantic validator, and tests must keep enum values aligned.

Architecture/governance docs must explain that this module prevents realtime/intermediary signals from becoming hidden delivery proof; it does not create a scheduler, browser recorder, subagent mailbox parser, or Codex runtime event bus.
acceptanceCriteria: IntermediaryReceiptCoverage has schemaVersion intermediary-receipt-coverage/v1 and records generatedAt, producer, runtimeStatus, evidenceUse, currentHeadSha, sources, claimPolicy, blockers, overallStatus, and nextAction.

Closed enum values for sourceKind, sourceStatus, freshness, evidenceUse, claimFamily, claimEligibility, blockerClass, overallStatus, and nextActionClass are pinned in TypeScript and schema; tests reject unknown values.
Validation accepts low-confidence unbound orientation only when intermediary source refs are pointer-only, redacted, non-raw, bounded, and explicitly ineligible for claim support.

Validation accepts higher-confidence receipt-bound orientation when intermediary source refs point to current receipts, but still marks the packet orientation-only unless claimPolicy explicitly allows a claim family.

Validation accepts claim-support eligibility only when the source is current, the linked evidence receipt is pass/current/claim_support, the receipt has verifiedAt, the head SHA matches when head-bound, and claimPolicy allows the requested claim family.
Validation rejects claim-support eligibility when the sourceKind and claimFamily pair is missing, unknown, partial, or not explicitly allowlisted in the claim policy matrix.

Validation rejects intermediary external_check_snapshot, mailbox_status, streamed_status, browser_state, compaction_summary, screenshot_or_visual_state, realtime_event_snippet, operator_steering_echo, and subagent_status_text sources as support for external-state, review-state, merge-readiness, Linear-alignment, or Judge/PM claim families unless they route through a canonical packet receipt from that family.

Validation applies most-restrictive-wins aggregation and rejects mixed-source conflicts where one source passes but another source for the same claim family is stale, head-mismatched, blocked, or policy-denied.
Validation includes at least one positive or negative fixture for every required sourceKind in the intermediary taxonomy.
Validation rejects blockers without a deterministic nextActionClass mapping and rejects unknown nextActionClass values.

Validation rejects claim-support eligibility from orientation-only evidence receipts, stale receipts, missing receipts, missing verifiedAt, missing or mismatched head SHA, stale source freshness, expired source TTL, and policy-disallowed claim families.

Validation rejects raw browser DOM dumps, screenshots/image bytes, transcripts, mailbox bodies, streamed tool payloads, compaction bodies, secret-like keys, secret-like scalar values, unknown top-level fields, and unknown nested fields.
Validation classifies stale realtime/intermediary sources as orientation_blocked or audit_only rather than pass for claim support.
Standalone script validation can validate the example fixture and return machine-readable JSON on failure.
Runtime packet schema manifest admits intermediary-receipt-coverage/v1 with schema, example, and semantic validator pointers.

Tests cover positive unbound orientation, positive receipt-bound orientation, positive claim support, deny-by-default matrix gaps, partial policy objects, canonical-packet-only claim families, orientation-only receipt rejected for claim support, stale receipt, missing verifiedAt, head mismatch, mixed-source conflict, policy-disallowed claim, expired source, raw payload rejection, secret rejection, unknown next action rejection, unknown enum rejection, per-source-kind taxonomy coverage, and schema parity.
The module is discoverable from ARCHITECTURE.md and agent-governance docs without requiring implementers to inspect broad command files.

Slice review artifacts include improve-codebase-architecture, simplify, unslopify, he-code-review, testing, adversarial-reviewer, agent-native-reviewer, and best-practices-researcher results or explicit blocked/not-applicable evidence before done is claimed.
validationPlan: pnpm exec biome check .harness/intent/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit-pu-038-spg-012-intermediary-receipt-coverage-intent.json src/lib/intermediary-receipts contracts/intermediary-receipt-coverage.schema.json contracts/examples/intermediary-receipt-coverage.example.json contracts/runtime-packet-schemas.manifest.json scripts/validate-intermediary-receipt-coverage.cjs src/dev/validate-runtime-packet-schemas-script.test.ts ARCHITECTURE.md AGENTS.md docs/agents/00-architecture-bootstrap.md docs/agents/07b-agent-governance.md

git diff --check -- .harness/intent/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit-pu-038-spg-012-intermediary-receipt-coverage-intent.json src/lib/intermediary-receipts contracts/intermediary-receipt-coverage.schema.json contracts/examples/intermediary-receipt-coverage.example.json contracts/runtime-packet-schemas.manifest.json scripts/validate-intermediary-receipt-coverage.cjs src/dev/validate-runtime-packet-schemas-script.test.ts ARCHITECTURE.md AGENTS.md docs/agents/00-architecture-bootstrap.md docs/agents/07b-agent-governance.md docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/state.yaml docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/receipts.jsonl .harness/active-artifacts.md artifacts/reviews/pu-038-spg-012-intermediary-receipt-coverage-*.md
rollbackPlan: Remove IntermediaryReceiptCoverage/v1 schema, example, validator, module, script, manifest entry, and tests.
Remove intermediary receipt coverage references from architecture and governance docs.

Preserve existing evidence-receipt, prompt-context, prompt-context-drift, runtime-card, delivery-truth, review-state, external-state, and PR closeout behavior unchanged unless a separate explicit wiring slice consumes the coverage packet.
currentStateNotes: Current branch is codex/jsc-363-intermediary-receipt-coverage at head 43aa212986e721f885fe7dffbf756b0b9703b9cb before this intent artifact.
PR #314 is green at the same head and remains a separate stacked PR truth lane; this slice starts only after PU-037 handoff was committed and pushed.
Untracked files already exist outside this slice and must not be staged, deleted, or used as proof for SPG-012.
~/.codex/.env is not read or sourced by this local contract slice unless live CircleCI or GitHub triage becomes necessary.
stopConditions: Stop if intermediary receipt coverage requires embedding raw browser state, transcripts, mailbox text, compaction bodies, streamed tool payloads, screenshots, secrets, tokens, or bulky telemetry.

Stop if realtime/intermediary state would be treated as merge_ready, review_addressed, Linear-aligned, goal-ready, Judge/PM-ready, or delivery-truth pass proof without current receipt-bound claim eligibility.

Stop if the implementation would replace existing evidence-receipt, prompt-context-drift, runtime-card, review-state, external-state, or delivery-truth contracts instead of adding a bounded coverage packet.
Stop if the design requires mutating Codex upstream, live PRs, Linear, CircleCI, CodeRabbit, release state, or merge state.
